title: Xenial Briny Norwegian Lamb Kebabs
description: A traditional Norwegian dish featuring ground lamb, cardamom, and a briny cheese pull, perfect for a comforting meal on a sick day. This recipe is low in sodium and can be cooked on a yakimono grill for a smoky flavor.
instructions: Preheat the yakimono grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, ground cardamom, salt-free seasoning blend, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined.
Thread the lamb mixture onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the lamb kebabs with olive oil and grill for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until cooked through to an internal temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the lamb is cooking, grill the chopped onion and bell pepper for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until tender and lightly charred.
Once the lamb is cooked, remove from the grill and top each kebab with crumbled feta cheese.
Return the kebabs to the grill and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the cheese is melted and slightly browned.
Serve the lamb kebabs hot, topped with grilled onion and bell pepper, and a squeeze of lemon juice.
